headline: 29% target met; strong teacher engagement but regional coverage gaps emerging.
collection_status: 393 of 1,350 responses collected (29%). Lindi and Mara regions leading (134 responses combined). Rukwa critically underrepresented at 9 responses; five regions below 35 responses each, indicating uneven rollout requiring acceleration.
key_findings: Teacher-led momentum: 201 of 393 (51%) are KiuFunza teachers (lower and upper), validating implementation credibility and grassroots buy-in.
Expansion appetite dominates: 139 respondents (35%) prioritise expanding KiuFunza; policy integration ranks third (83), indicating stakeholders see scale as prerequisite to sustainability.
Scale readiness gap: Average 2.7/5 readiness score signals operational bottlenecks despite 4.4/5 partner satisfaction—systems and capacity lag strategy appetite.

Qualitative signals consistent: Feedback emphasises teacher incentives, digital monitoring, government partnership, and programme expansion—aligns with quantitative priorities but reveals resource/sustainability concerns.
flags: Regional imbalance: Rukwa (9), Singida (25), Songwe (29) severely undersampled; risk of skewed findings and unrepresentative policy input from southern/western zones.
Scale readiness 2.7/5 is critical: High expansion demand paired with low operational readiness suggests respondents want growth without believing systems can support it—implementation risk.
Respondent type skew: 153 lower-primary teachers dominate; only 6 DEOs and 2 REOs suggest weak district/regional authority engagement, limiting buy-in for scaling.
recommendation: Immediately intensify collection in Rukwa, Singida, Songwe, and Manyara via targeted phone surveys and regional field visits (target +200 responses in 2 weeks). Simultaneously conduct rapid diagnostic with DEOs/REOs on scale readiness barriers—prioritise this over pursuit of 1,350 target until operational bottlenecks are mapped.
